ogTitle: Seal Rocks vs Sedgefield: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Seal Rocks and Sedgefield.
heading: Seal Rocks vs Sedgefield — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Seal Rocks, New South Wales and Sedgefield, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sedgefield has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Seal Rocks (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Seal Rocks or Sedgefield safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Seal Rocks has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Sedgefield scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Seal Rocks scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Sedgefield scores 38/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Seal Rocks or Sedgefield?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sedgefield has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
